description: Port forward to sandbox gateway is fragile. openshell forward uses sandbox supervisor PID1 internal proxy. Processes started via kubectl exec bypass this routing.
resolution: Use FIFO pipe to feed commands into openshell sandbox connect session so gateway starts through the supervisor.

title: Browser auto-install via Playwright in onboard step 4
rationale: Ubuntu 24.04 chromium-browser is snap stub. Playwright downloads real binary. Non-fatal so onboard continues on failure.

description: Writing to /sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json from host (via openshell sandbox upload) updates overlay upperdir but sandbox processes still see stale file from VFS page cache. Gateway starts with old config, browser flag not picked up.
resolution: After uploading the modified file, run echo 3 > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches on host (inside Docker) to flush page cache, then restart gateway. Also ensure /sandbox/.openclaw/ is owned by uid 998 (sandbox user) — gateway writes temp config files on startup and fails silently if permission denied.

content: Enable chromium in sandbox: patch /sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json to add browser:{enabled:true, executablePath:/opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell, noSandbox:true, headless:true}. Binary is baked into image — never download at runtime (deny-by-default blocks cdn.playwright.dev). Gateway exposes browser control on port 18791 when enabled. After config change: update sha256 at .openclaw/.config-hash, chown uid 998, drop VFS cache, restart gateway. See cli/bin/lib/onboard.js:784-842.
